rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FELIX ZAMUDIO JUAREZ 2010 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/26. (No earlier availability.) 1) It appears that the trustee paid his attorney twice for services provided and costs incurred (although not the exact amount of costs ordered) in the conservatorship case, per the order dated 6/1/22 (and filed on 6/2/22). The second accounting showed payments on 3/17/23. This accounting shows payments on 4/13/23. 2) Attorney fees previously authorized (order dated 6/1/22) and caregiver fees were not on the liabilities schedule in the second accounting. 3) There was over $4,788 paid to a third party to prepare the accounting plus monthly costs for Quickbooks. Is the trustee not maintaining the trust financial records himself? 4) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. 5) Was Patricia Juarez not survived by issue? 6) Who is or was Blanca R. Juarez? When did she die? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/6/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ROBERT DONJACOUR 2019 TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 4, 2020
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the number of shares of securities must be shown in an accounting. It is NOT standard for any schedule showing assets on hand not to include the number of shares of securities. The values of securities fluctuate, but the number of shares is specific, as of a date certain, as shown on Schedule E. Transfer of cash between accounts is NOT a change in the form of assets. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] The amendment could have submitted just the one missing schedule instead of the entire accounting again.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order, there being no objection, although (1) the 13100 affidavit was still incomplete, despite the reminder in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 10/14/25 to include the language required by Probate Code Section 13101(a)(5), (2) the petition caption did not disclose that additional attorney fees and costs were being sought, as required by CRC 7.102, and (3) there is no authority for an open-ended order authorizing additional, undisclosed legal fees (a fiduciary may request a reserve, which Conservator did not do here). Court investigators are not "interested persons" to whom notice needs to be given. (They, like probate examiners, should just be provided courtesy copies of filed papers in order to do their investigations or reviews.) Supplements are not amendments. [CRC 7.3.] The title on the caption of the order on the final account or final report on waiver of account must begin with Order Terminating Conservatorship. [LRSF 14.47 F.3.e.] The footer of a pleading must contain the title of the paper or some clear and concise abbreviation. [CRC 2.110.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KIRA S PLETTSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/27/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Item 3f(2) of the petition has the wrong date of the will. (3) The will identifies the decedent's daughter as Elena Prettser, not Plettser. (4) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (5) If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. {LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (6) Who are the heirs-at-law of the predeceased husband? The first box under 7b(1) or (2) of the petition must be checked and all such heirs of Alexander Plettser must be listed in item 8 of the petition. (7) Item 9 of the petition is blank. (8) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? (9) If the trustee is the petitioner, all beneficiaries of the trust must be listed in item 8 and must receive notice. (10) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (11) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. An amended petition must be filed, addressing all these issues, including the correct date of the will. After the amended petition is e-filed, notice of it must be mailed to all heirs and must also be published. A proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition and all other papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/13/26 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
